Patch Set 4:

Except for the paragraph added at the top I don't see the point of these comments. We shouldn't add tautological doxygen like this.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(18 comments)

John, on the one hand, you were objecting in patch #1 to expanding the comments to provide further explanation (e.g. what they are used for), yet you also object to the terse comments that you asked for.

I agree that a comment "Setup LWP device" for "lwp_setup()" is not very useful.  Having the description of the function arguments (e.g. what "nidstring" is actually describing) adds more value to the comment blocks.  

For wrapper functions that are only a few lines long I don't think we need to have comment blocks for them.  There is some value even for sparse comment blocks on large functions, firstly to have a place to accumulate comments in the future, and secondly as a good practice to get people in the habit of adding comments to new code and this is easier to enforce if we are consistent about it.

The -1 is for other issues I found when reviewing the comments.

Patch Set 4:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/cd88ba48-0d31-11e4-ae0c-5254006e85c2).  Ran 19 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 4:

> John, on the one hand, you were objecting in patch #1 to expanding the comments to provide further explanation (e.g. what they are used for), yet you also object to the terse comments that you asked for.

I wish that they would focus on explaining what *this* function does.

I didn't ask for terse comments. I said that the brief description should be as terse as possible, By the brief description I mean the first line of the block. It should be of the form "Declare a LWP unlink bojangle" rather than a four part run on sentence about LWP, OSTs, MDTs, and bojangles. After the brief description there's no need to be terse provided that it's something worth reading.

Second I would like it if these focused on explaining what this function does. Rather than saying that it is a certain way because of some other function two layers up that may have been deleted by now.

Patch Set 4:

(4 comments)

Thanks for the review, Andreas.

parents.subject: LU-5502 lod: add a high-level description of the LOD layer

commit.subject: LU-4976 osp: add comments for lwp_dev.c functions
commit.message:
LU-4976 osp: add comments for lwp_dev.c functions

Add introductory comment block for the lwp_dev.c file.
Add function comment blocks to all functions in the lwp_dev.c file.

Signed-off-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I8a672982964a742d56b1d64e7b8cceb31aa64a48
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10335
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Ned Bass <bass6@llnl.gov>
Reviewed-by: Richard Henwood <richard.henwood@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
